title: The Science of Nutrition
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Our (0) scientific understanding of nutrition has advanced enormously in recent decades. We now know that a (17) varied diet containing plenty of fresh produce is essential for maintaining good health. Nutritionists recommend limiting the (18) consumption of ultra-processed foods, which are often high in sugar, salt, and (19) unhealthy fats.||Research has revealed that the gut microbiome plays a crucial role in overall health and (20) immunity. What we eat directly affects our mood, energy levels, and (21) cognitive function. Despite this (22) growing body of evidence, many people find it (23) difficult to change their eating habits due to busy lifestyles, food marketing, and limited access to (24) affordable fresh produce.

example.text: Our (0) SCIENTIFIC understanding of nutrition has advanced enormously in recent decades. (SCIENCE)

questions.explanation: The adjective 'varied' is formed from 'vary' by replacing '-y' with '-ied'.
The noun 'consumption' is formed from 'consume' by replacing '-me' with '-mption'.
The adjective 'unhealthy' is formed from 'health' by adding the prefix 'un-' and the suffix '-y'.
The noun 'immunity' is formed from 'immune' by replacing '-e' with '-ity'.
The adjective 'cognitive' is formed from 'cognition' by replacing '-ition' with '-ive'.
The gerund/adjective 'growing' is formed from 'grow' by adding the suffix '-ing'.
The adjective 'difficult' is the base form; 'difficulty' is the noun formed by adding '-y'.
The adjective 'affordable' is formed from 'afford' by adding the suffix '-able'.
